title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope in Cancer Fight
subtitle: Recent clinical trials reveal innovative pills that help the body fight tumors more effectively.

summary.en: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. These new drugs work by helping the immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. One specific pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. Another treatment helps patients with various common cancers by removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that tumors use to hide. While these results are early, experts believe they represent a significant step forward in oncology.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員近期在芝加哥的一場大型會議上，分享了癌症治療的激勵人心新進展。這些新藥物的作用機制是協助免疫系統發現並摧毀先前隱藏的癌細胞。其中一種藥錠已展現出能使胰臟癌患者存活時間翻倍的能力。另一種治療方式則透過移除腫瘤用來躲避偵測的「隱形斗篷」，協助多種常見癌症患者。雖然這些結果仍處於早期階段，但專家認為這代表腫瘤學領域向前邁出了重要的一步。

paragraphs.en: Medical experts and researchers recently gathered in Chicago to share major updates in the fight against cancer. Building on the progress reported earlier this month, these new findings highlight how modern science is creating smarter ways to treat the disease. By moving beyond traditional methods, doctors are finding ways to help the body’s own immune system recognize and attack cancer cells that were once impossible to see.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學專家與研究人員近期齊聚芝加哥，分享對抗癌症的重大進展。這些新發現建立在本月稍早發布的成果之上，凸顯了現代科學如何創造出更聰明的治療疾病方式。透過超越傳統療法，醫師們正設法協助人體自身的免疫系統，去辨識並攻擊那些過去難以察覺的癌細胞。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, a new experimental tablet called GRWD5769 has shown impressive results in patients who had previously failed other treatments. Many of these patients had no other options left before joining the study. The drug works by removing what researchers call 'invisibility cloaks' from tumor cells. Once these cloaks are removed, the body’s immune system can detect the cancer and destroy it. In a trial involving patients from countries including the UK, Spain, and Australia, the drug helped shrink tumors in several common types of cancer, such as lung, bowel, and liver cancer.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，一種名為 GRWD5769 的新型實驗性藥錠，在先前嘗試其他治療失敗的患者身上展現了令人印象深刻的成果。許多患者在參與這項研究前已別無選擇。該藥物的作用機制是移除研究人員所稱的腫瘤細胞「隱形斗篷」。一旦這些斗篷被移除，人體的免疫系統就能偵測到癌症並將其摧毀。在一項涵蓋英國、西班牙與澳洲等國患者的試驗中，該藥物協助縮小了多種常見癌症的腫瘤，例如肺癌、腸癌與肝癌。

paragraphs.en: Prof Fiona Thistlethwaite, the lead researcher for this trial, noted that it is very impressive to see such results from a simple tablet. While she emphasized that it is still early and more studies are needed, she believes this new mechanism clearly helps immunotherapy—a type of treatment that uses the body's immune system to fight disease—perform much more effectively. In another study presented at the conference, a different smart drug called ivonescimab was shown to help lung cancer patients live 15% longer on average when combined with chemotherapy.
paragraphs.zh: 這項試驗的首席研究員費歐娜·希斯索斯韋特（Fiona Thistlethwaite）教授指出，能從一顆簡單的藥錠看到如此成果非常驚人。雖然她強調目前仍處於早期階段，還需要更多研究，但她相信這種新機制顯然有助於免疫療法——一種利用人體免疫系統對抗疾病的治療方式——發揮更顯著的效用。在會議中發表的另一項研究顯示，另一種名為 ivonescimab 的智慧藥物，在與化學治療合併使用時，平均能協助肺癌患者延長百分之十五的壽命。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps the most significant news shared in Chicago concerns pancreatic cancer, which is often described as the world’s deadliest common cancer. For years, there have been very few effective treatments for this condition. However, a new pill called daraxonrasib has changed the outlook for many patients. In a trial of 500 people, this pill doubled the average survival time compared to traditional chemotherapy. Patients taking the new drug lived for an average of 13.2 months, while those on chemotherapy lived for less than seven months. Dr. Rachna Shroff, an expert in gastrointestinal cancers at the University of Arizona Cancer Center, described these results as 'landscape-changing' for the medical field.
paragraphs.zh: 在芝加哥分享的消息中，或許最重大的進展與胰臟癌有關，這種癌症常被形容為全球最致命的常見癌症。多年來，針對此病症有效的治療方式寥寥無幾。然而，一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥錠改變了許多患者的前景。在一項五百人的試驗中，該藥錠將平均存活時間較傳統化學治療提升了一倍。服用新藥的患者平均存活時間為十三點二個月，而接受化學治療的患者存活時間則不到七個月。亞利桑那大學癌症中心（University of Arizona Cancer Center）的胃腸道癌症專家拉赫娜·史洛夫（Rachna Shroff）醫師，將這些結果形容為醫學領域「改變地景」的突破。

paragraphs.en: Another innovative approach involves a drug named ozekibart. This treatment mimics a natural protein in the body to bind to specific parts of cancer cells. By doing this, the drug triggers the death of the cancer cells while causing very little harm to healthy tissue. Patients who received this treatment reported that it helped stop their cancer from growing, with some even describing the therapy as giving them a 'new lease of life.'
paragraphs.zh: 另一種創新方法涉及一種名為 ozekibart 的藥物。這種治療方式模仿人體內的天然蛋白質，以結合癌細胞的特定部位。透過這種方式，該藥物能觸發癌細胞死亡，同時對健康組織造成的傷害極小。接受此治療的患者表示，這有助於抑制癌症生長，有些人甚至形容這項療法給了他們「重獲新生」的機會。

paragraphs.en: These developments are part of a larger trend in medicine where drugs are becoming more targeted. Instead of using broad treatments that affect the whole body, these new 'smart' drugs focus specifically on the cancer cells. This approach often leads to fewer side effects for the patient. Cancer Research UK has called these discoveries a promising step forward in the ongoing war against the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 這些發展是醫學界更大趨勢的一部分，即藥物正變得越來越具標靶性。這些新型「智慧」藥物不再使用影響全身的廣泛性治療，而是專注於癌細胞本身。這種方法通常能減少患者的副作用。英國癌症研究協會（Cancer Research UK）稱這些發現是持續對抗癌症過程中，充滿希望的一大步。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement, experts remain cautious and emphasize that these are still early days for many of these treatments. Further research and larger studies are necessary to confirm these findings and ensure the drugs are safe for long-term use. Nevertheless, the mood among the medical community is one of optimism. By finding new ways to expose and destroy tumors, scientists are providing patients with more options and, most importantly, more time. As these trials continue, the medical world will be watching closely to see how these treatments perform in the real world outside of clinical studies.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管令人振奮，專家仍保持謹慎，並強調這些治療方式大多仍處於早期階段。未來需要進行更深入的研究與更大規模的試驗，以確認這些發現並確保藥物長期使用的安全性。儘管如此，醫學界仍充滿樂觀氣氛。透過尋找揭露並摧毀腫瘤的新方法，科學家正為患者提供更多選擇，最重要的是，爭取到更多時間。隨著這些試驗持續進行，醫學界將密切關注這些治療方式在臨床研究之外的現實世界中表現如何。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To not be helped or cured by a medical treatment.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （醫療上）治療無效；對某種治療沒有反應。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「失敗」，這裡指患者對之前的治療沒有反應。文中：a new experimental tablet called GRWD5769 has shown impressive results in patients who had previously failed other treatments.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The likely future situation or prospect for someone or something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「觀點」，這裡指病情的預後或未來發展。文中：However, a new pill called daraxonrasib has changed the outlook for many patients.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To attach or stick to something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「綁」，這裡指藥物分子與細胞結合。文中：This treatment mimics a natural protein in the body to bind to specific parts of cancer cells.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Removing ‘invisibility cloaks’ and safely skipping chemo: new weapons in war on cancer shared at US conference

previousArticles.title: New Advances Bring Hope in the Fight Against Cancer
previousArticles.summaryEn: Recent medical conferences and studies have revealed major progress in cancer treatment. Scientists have developed new smart drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for patients. Surgical research suggests that choosing the right method can significantly lower the risk of cervical cancer returning. However, experts warn that a global shortage of healthcare workers could create future challenges for cancer care.

quiz.question: What is the specific function of the experimental drug GRWD5769 according to the text?

options.A: It directly kills cancer cells by acting as a form of chemotherapy.
options.B: It exposes tumor cells by removing their ability to remain hidden from the immune system.
options.C: It replaces the body's immune system with a more effective synthetic version.
options.D: It prevents the growth of new blood vessels that feed lung and liver tumors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，該藥物的作用是移除腫瘤細胞上的「隱形斗篷」（invisibility cloaks），使免疫系統能偵測並摧毀癌細胞。選項 A 錯誤，因為該藥物是透過免疫系統而非直接化療作用；選項 C 錯誤，因為它只是幫助免疫系統，並非取代；選項 D 雖然提到肺癌與肝癌，但並未提及血管生長機制。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might doctors prefer using 'smart' drugs over traditional cancer treatments?

options.A: Smart drugs are guaranteed to cure all types of cancer completely.
options.B: Traditional treatments are no longer available in most medical facilities.
options.C: Smart drugs are designed to be more precise, potentially reducing damage to healthy parts of the body.
options.D: Smart drugs are less expensive to produce than traditional chemotherapy.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段提到，這些「智慧型」藥物趨勢是針對性（targeted）治療，而非影響全身的廣泛性治療，這導致患者的副作用較少。選項 A 過於誇大，文中僅提到「更有希望」而非保證治癒；選項 B 文中未提及傳統治療消失；選項 D 文中並未討論藥物的生產成本。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'landscape-changing' mean in the context of the medical field?

options.A: Physically altering the geography of the hospital grounds.
options.B: Creating a significant and positive shift in how a condition is treated.
options.C: Replacing old doctors with new, younger medical professionals.
options.D: Increasing the amount of paperwork required for cancer patients.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「landscape-changing」是用來形容 daraxonrasib 藥物對胰臟癌治療帶來的重大突破，意指改變了該領域的現況或治療標準。選項 A 是字面上的誤解；選項 C 與 D 皆與醫學治療的突破無關，屬於不相關的干擾項。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding modern cancer research?

options.A: Traditional chemotherapy is now considered completely ineffective and should be abandoned.
options.B: Pancreatic cancer is the only area of oncology that has seen recent scientific progress.
options.C: Medical experts are optimistic about new, targeted therapies that improve patient outcomes and survival time.
options.D: International cooperation is the only factor responsible for the success of recent drug trials.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於介紹多項新的「智慧型」藥物（如 GRWD5769、daraxonrasib、ozekibart），並強調這些進展為患者帶來了更多存活時間與治療希望，且醫學界對此持樂觀態度。選項 A 過於極端，文中並未否定傳統治療；選項 B 錯誤，因為文章也提到了肺癌與其他癌症；選項 D 雖然提到了國際合作，但這並非文章的主旨。
